authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 112 - Standards for the Growing, Harvesting, Packing, and Holding of Produce for Human Consumption
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal requirements for covered produce operations, handling controls, field and sanitation workflows, water-related control expectations, and required recordkeeping in crop operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: initial intake validation, operation continuity checks, and evidence sufficiency review
used_for: operations sequencing where records drive state progression

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S - Additional Traceability Records for Certain Foods
authoritative_sources.source_class: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines federal traceability record expectations, including what records must exist for forward/reverse movement and retrieval timing.

authoritative_sources.title: Frequently Asked Questions: FSMA Food Traceability Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official interpretive implementation source for traceability applicability and practical handling of critical tracking events.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: source conflict evaluation and ambiguity handling

authoritative_sources.title: Worker Protection Standard FAQ (40 CFR Part 170)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official source clarifying pesticide handling obligations and worker-safety conditions that may constrain field sequencing and execution state progression.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: environmental/operational risk gate and exception handling

authoritative_sources.title: NPDES Permit Basics
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Vendor-neutral environmental permitting workflow reference used to identify when discharge/runoff context changes execution safety boundaries.

authoritative_sources.title: Conservation Crop Rotation (Ac.) (328) Conservation Practice Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Land use guidance with operational implications for season timing, crop rotation decisions, and sequencing constraints in field operations.

authoritative_sources.title: 5025: Commingling and Contamination Prevention in Organic Production and Handling
authoritative_sources.source_class: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Direct operational guidance for commingling controls, contamination prevention, and handling claims where organic integrity and lot separation matter.

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Traceability Standard
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Industry-recognized vocabulary and event structure for translating cross-system traceability and custody terminology safely.

workflow_stages.name: intake and qualification
workflow_stages.description: Collect crop scope, jurisdictional context, and records-of-truth before deciding scope.
source_classes: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references

workflow_stages.name: seasonal and land dependency screening
workflow_stages.description: Evaluate seasonality, weather, land-use rotation, and seasonal constraints before state progression.
source_classes: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards

workflow_stages.name: evidence and traceability assembly
workflow_stages.description: Validate field plans, logs, seasonal records, lot lineage, and custody transitions before execution or completion claims.
source_classes: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references

workflow_stages.name: environmental and food-safety dependency handling
workflow_stages.description: Assess pesticide, runoff, contamination, and permit-adjacent exposure risks before continuing or closing tasks.

workflow_stages.name: exception escalation and completion packaging
workflow_stages.description: Generate explicit blocker lists, state transitions, and routing notes when evidence or authority is incomplete.
source_classes: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references

artifacts: environmental event reports and permit references

allowed: Classify requests and set execution state based on evidence readiness.
Detect seasonal and environmental constraints before completion.
Prepare completion records with explicit blockers, artifacts, and next actions.
Route to adjacent specialists when authority boundary is crossed.
escalate: Missing source-of-truth records that change workflow truth set.
Environmental, WPS, runoff, or food-safety risk that requires specialist intervention.
Requests implying agronomic, veterinary, legal, or permit determinations.
refuse: Any request to complete operations without required records and seasonal safety checks.
Any request to provide final permit, legal, agronomic, veterinary, or release signoff.
Any attempt to bypass unresolved contradictions across land, traceability, or environmental evidence.

domain_failure_modes: Marking completion despite missing seasonal planning, field proof, or traceability artifacts.
Ignoring runoff/waste, WPS, or land-rotation constraints and continuing execution.
Mismatched handoff records causing lot-level contamination or custody errors.
Failing to escalate requests that require agronomic, veterinary, environmental, or legal authority.
Treating tenant policy conflicts as resolved when applicable federal or permit context differs.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Seasonal, land, or traceability dependency gap
Request that implies agronomic, veterinary, or environmental signoff

unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_plan: Collect state permit scope and tenant-specific compliance owner before final completion decisions.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Exact crop-specification land-rest management policy for mixed-use land
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_plan: Require tenant policy override map at runtime; escalate to agronomy or environmental specialist where policies conflict.
